responses.text: The target article summarizes the internet career of Belle Delphine, focusing on her viral sales of "gamer girl bathwater" to her fans in 2019.
The English model and internet personality truly did offer jars of her bathwater for $30 each in July 2019.
She explicitly stated in an interview with Newsweek that she sold out of her initial stock, which she estimated to be around 500 units.
The article addresses widespread online rumors claiming that people had contracted herpes after drinking the bathwater.
This rumor was definitively a hoax, originating from a Twitter user impersonating the Daily Mail website.
Fact-checkers like Snopes confirmed that the rumor about a herpes outbreak was completely fabricated.
The Twitter impersonator later admitted they created the fake news as a prank, and Delphine firmly refuted the viral claims regarding the unsent products.
